subject.title: Mathematics Analysis and Approaches (AA)
subject.description:
**IB Mathematics Analysis and Approaches** is a **Group 5** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **number and algebra, functions, geometry and trigonometry, statistics and probability, and calculus**, with a strong focus on **theoretical understanding, formal mathematical reasoning, and analytical problem-solving**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2021**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | Non-calculator written exam | 90 minutes | 120 minutes | 40% |
| **Paper 2** | Calculator allowed written exam | 90 minutes | 120 minutes | 40% |
| **Paper 3** | Calculator allowed problem-solving | N/A | 60 minutes | 20% (HL only) |
| **Mathematical Exploration** | Internal Assessment | 20 hours | 20 hours | 20% |

strengths: Clear and concise title that effectively conveys the focus of the exploration.
Use of headings, sub-headings and a table of contents indicates an attempt at logical structure.
Ideas generally follow a cause-and-effect order, allowing the reader to follow the argument.
weaknesses: Long blocks of raw tables interrupt the flow and weaken coherence.
Table of contents is overly nested with inconsistent page numbering.
Excessive inclusion of raw data and step-by-step arithmetic reduces conciseness.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Strand assessments place coherence and organization at band 2 (some coherence, adequate structure) and conciseness at band 1 (some irrelevant material). A score of 2 accurately reflects âsome coherenceâ with basic structure, despite notable interruptions and unnecessary detail.

strengths: Use of standard symbols (Î£, r, xÌ, yÌ) and inclusion of clear figure captions that credit the author.
Computed mean values are highlighted in display math, aiding communication of key results.
weaknesses: Notation is inconsistent (variables mis-typed, decimal commas instead of points).
Tables and graphs lack clear labels and formatting, reducing interpretability.
Logical structure is weak: duplicate mean calculations and compressed derivations hinder clarity.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Although there is some use of mathematical symbols and representations (band 2), the limited logical structure and persistent notation errors place this work at band 1. A score of 1 reflects âlimitedâ communication despite occasional strengths.

strengths: The personal rationale clearly connects the studentâs own focus habits to the research question.
Reflection on applying two statistical methods shows engagement with real-world application.
weaknesses: Mathematical methods chosen are entirely conventional with no exploration of alternatives.
The overall structure follows a generic template, limiting evidence of true independent thinking.
Creativity and initiative are minimal, confined to standard statistical procedures.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Each strand is assessed at band 1 (limited independent thinking, some personal approach, some creativity). A score of 1 reflects the modest level of personal engagement demonstrated.

strengths: The comparative statement linking Pearsonâs and Spearmanâs coefficients shows reflective insight.
weaknesses: Reflection on the mathematical process is minimal and primarily descriptive.
Analysis of results is basic, restating thresholds without deeper critique.
Evaluation of outcomes offers only a brief list of limitations and a single extension.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Strand assessments identify only limited reflection, basic analysis and limited evaluation (all band 1). A score of 1 aligns with the minimal depth and critical insight provided.

strengths: Appropriate selection and application of Pearsonâs r and Spearmanâs Ï, both within the SL syllabus.
Mathematics used is fully commensurate with course level and applied correctly in principle.
Final r calculation is presented in clear block equations, demonstrating correct technique.
weaknesses: Arithmetic errors in the summation of squared deviations and duplicate mean computations indicate partial understanding.
No higher-level inference or advanced statistical methods beyond core tools.
Some formula applications (e.g. Spearmanâs Î£dÂ²) appear to misuse values, reducing accuracy.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Relevance and level appropriateness are at band 3 (relevant and commensurate mathematics), while understanding and accuracy sit at band 2 due to non-trivial errors. A final score of 3 reflects this mix of correct method selection with partial execution and calculation mistakes.

strandSelections.reasoning: Calculations are listed, but logical argument is hard to follow: the means are computed twice with different numbers, and the derivation of Î£dÂ² is compressed into unreadable strings. Consequently, clarity is limited and the internal logic of the procedure is difficult to trace, fitting band 1.

The student demonstrates the core formulae and completes lengthy manual computations, indicating some understanding; however, many arithmetic strings are inconsistent (e.g., duplicate mean calculations, Î£dÂ² mis-summations), signalling non-trivial errors. Hence understanding is only partial, fitting band 2.

Tables, a scatter plot and a significance graph are provided, plus algebraic expressions, satisfying âsome use of different forms of representation.â Yet tables are poorly formatted and the scatter plot lacks axis labels for units, so the representations are not fully effective.

The exploration follows a recognisable sequence (rationale â introduction â procedure â results â evaluation), and ideas generally proceed in a cause-and-effect order, allowing the reader to follow the argument. However, long blocks of raw tables interrupt the flow, and several computational digressions appear without explanation, so coherence is frequently weakened. Overall the work shows âsome coherenceâ rather than the consistently smooth progression required for a higher band.

Using two correlation measures is standard practice and no innovative modelling or data treatment is attempted. The study design and presentation do not show notable creativity, so the strand remains in the lowest non-zero band.

Discussion of correlation strength and significance is essentially a restatement of thresholds from secondary sources; there is no deeper critique of sampling, ranking method, or calculation accuracy. This corresponds to âbasic analysis of results.â

Outcomes are acknowledged (âweak positive correlationâ and some limitations), but there is little exploration of implications or future research beyond a single suggested extension. This matches the rubric description of a âlimited evaluation of outcomes.â

The student provides a brief conclusion and a bullet-point limitations table, but reflection on the mathematical process or the meaning of results is minimal. Thus only âlimited reflectionâ is evident.

The student uses mathematical symbols (Î£, r, xÌ, yÌ) and formulae for Pearson and Spearman, but many variables are mis-typed (e.g. xi-x shown as â(xi - mean of x)â in plain text) and commas are used instead of decimal points. This meets âsome appropriate language and notationâ but falls short of mostly consistent accuracy.

The rationale links the investigation to the studentâs experience, demonstrating a personal connection; however, the remainder of the work follows a generic template without distinctive personal decision-making. Hence only âsome evidence of a personal approachâ is present.

Large amounts of repetitive raw data, partial screen-captures, and step-by-step arithmetic that could have been summarised clutter the report. These inclusions are only marginally relevant to the readerâs understanding and reduce conciseness, matching the descriptor that âthe exploration contains some irrelevant material.â

Pearsonâs r and Spearmanâs Ï are both appropriate statistical tools for assessing linear and monotonic association, respectively, within the course syllabus. The mathematics chosen therefore is clearly relevant and applied in context, warranting band 3 for relevance.

Selecting a personally relevant topic and collecting original data shows some ownership, but mathematical choices (standard textbook statistics) were entirely conventional and no alternative methods were considered. Evidence of independent thinking is therefore limited.

Headings, sub-headings and a table of contents indicate an attempt at structure, yet the section order inside the âMathematical Procedureâ is confusing and several tables span pages without captions or numbering. The document is therefore âadequately organised with basic structureâ, but not âwell organised with clear structureâ as required for band 3.

Both correlation techniques and basic significance comparison are firmly within SL level expectations and used correctly in principle. No higher-level statistical inference or calculus is attempted, so âcommensurate with course levelâ (band 3) is appropriate.

annotations.comment: The title is clear and concise, effectively conveying the focus of the exploration and engaging the reader from the start.
The introductory paragraph lacks context on the target population and scope. Clarify the sampleâs demographics and any inclusion criteria to strengthen the rationale.
The table of contents is overly nested and lacks consistent page numbering. Simplify headings, ensure accurate page references, and remove duplicated elements for clarity.
The studentâs personal rationale vividly connects their own focus habits to the research question, demonstrating clear evidence of a personal approach.
The transition from rationale to hypothesis is abrupt. Reflect on how the personal context informed hypothesis formulation to deepen critical analysis.
Including the full raw data table interrupts the flow and is excessive. Summarize key statistics and move raw data to an appendix to improve conciseness.
The figure caption is succinct and credits the author appropriately, supporting clear mathematical communication.
The formula for Pearsonâs r is correctly stated, showing appropriate choice of relevant mathematics.
The summation of deviations is presented in a long inline string. Break the calculation into named steps or use a table to improve readability.
The computed mean value is highlighted in display math, which clearly communicates the result; however, confirm that the numerator is correctly simplified.
The correlation strength categories omit comparison symbols (e.g., â¤0.39). Include inequality symbols to define ranges precisely.
Tables of deviations are numbered and labeled inconsistently, with missing column headers. Standardize formatting and include titles for each table.
The sum of squares table is exhaustive but could be condensed by calculating totals directly. A summary row with Î£(xâxÌ)Â² and Î£(yâÈ³)Â² would suffice.
Embedding the equation label within the figure caption reduces clarity. Present the mathematical expression separately, with a clear reference label.
The long inline product series for Î£(xâxÌ)(yâÈ³) is difficult to parse. Use a stepped layout or bullet list to separate individual terms.
Decimal commas are used inconsistently, which can confuse readers. Replace commas with decimal points throughout to adhere to standard mathematical notation.
The final r calculation is clearly presented in block equations, demonstrating accurate use of mathematics at SL level.
Interpretation correctly recognizes a weak correlation, but discussion of practical implications or limitations at this stage would deepen reflection.
The Spearman p-value calculation appears to misuse Î£dÂ². Verify the total before applying in the formula to ensure accuracy.
The comparative statement linking both coefficients is well made, demonstrating substantive reflection on agreement between methods.
The significance graph lacks axis labels and units, reducing interpretability. Add clear axis titles and scale annotations to improve communication.
The discussion of anomalies in the scatter plot highlights insight into data behavior, showing thoughtful engagement with outliers.
The conclusion contradicts earlier certainty by expressing doubt about the correlation. Ensure logical coherence by aligning conclusion with interpretation consistently.
The evaluation tableâs second bullet trails off without completion. Expand on how differing definitions of productivity impacted data accuracy.
The extensions and learning reflections table is dense and could be broken into distinct sections with clear subheadings for readability.
The references are mixed in style and punctuation. Adopt a consistent citation format (e.g., MLA or APA) throughout for professional presentation.
The authorâs reflection on applying two statistical methods demonstrates significant personal engagement and real-world application of course concepts.
